text.title: AI-Powered PM Tools (Project Manager) — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every AI-PM-tools buyer's decision record must enforce. The rules pin the discipline that converts vendor-pitch noise into a defensible selection at any audit.

rule.statement: The decision record carries five required fields: incumbent platform, AI-tier price delta, integration fit score against current stack, ROI gate threshold, and a named buyer's sign-off. Records missing any field are rejected at procurement review.
rule.rationale: Selections without traceable rationale rot at renewal cycles. A signed five-field record makes the choice defensible at audit and at re-evaluation.

rule.statement: Evaluate the incumbent vendor's AI tier first. A switch to a different platform requires the incumbent uplift to fail the ROI gate by ≥20%; smaller margins are absorbed by switching costs.
rule.rationale: Switching costs (data migration, retraining, integration rewiring) typically consume 12-18 months of any uplift gain. The 20% rule prevents AI-feature FOMO from churning the stack.

rule.statement: Integration inventory (SSO, audit-log sink, Git, calendar, comms connectors) MUST be confirmed against the vendor support matrix before contract signature. "Coming soon" connectors are treated as absent.
rule.rationale: Integration gaps surface 3-6 months post-purchase when the rollout stalls. Pre-signature confirmation prevents hidden "we'll write a custom integration" expense.

rule.statement: Run a 60-day pilot before full rollout. Pilot measures cost-per-hour-saved; selections where this exceeds the team's blended hourly rate are rejected.
rule.rationale: Marketing-claimed uplift rarely survives a measured pilot. Forcing a cost-per-hour-saved gate prevents paying brand premiums that do not materialise.

rule.statement: AI features that mutate the PM source of truth are disabled by default and re-enabled only after a 30-day shadow run logs zero incorrect mutations against a labelled sample.
rule.rationale: Vendor "smart automation" frequently mis-handles edge cases (renamed projects, custom statuses). Shadow runs catch this without corrupting live data.

example.title: Five-field decision record
example.code: incumbent: Jira ai_tier_delta: +40%/seat (Rovo) integration_fit_score: 4/4 roi_gate_threshold: $45/h (blended rate) pilot_result: $38/h saved → passes gate buyer: vpe:viktoria decision: enable Rovo; shadow-run all auto-mutations for 30d.
example.note: Satisfies r1, r3, r4, r5. Incumbent evaluated first per r2.

example.title: Bypass switching cost rule
example.code: incumbent: Jira proposed: Linear AI roi_delta: incumbent +35%, Linear +50% (margin 15%) decision: switch to Linear
example.note: Violates r2: margin under 20% is absorbed by switching cost; should have stayed on incumbent.
